message:
feat(tests): add pure-function tests for dashboard helpers

Add tests for internal/tui/dashboard improving coverage from 45.3% to 46.3%:

- helpers_test.go: Tests for formatAgeShort, copyTimeMap, refreshDue,
  activityLabelAndColor, activityBadge, activityCountBadge

All tests are pure-function, table-driven, using t.Parallel() per project conventions.

Closes bd-1ud3q

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1u462","title":"TOON: Add [robot.output] config section","description":"Add configuration for robot mode output format.

## Config Schema

```toml
[robot.output]
# Default output format: json or toon
format = "json"

# Pretty print output (adds whitespace for readability)
pretty = false

# Include timestamps in output
timestamps = true

# Compression (for large outputs)
compress = false
```

## Implementation Location
- Update: internal/config/config.go
- Update: internal/config/defaults.go

## Struct Definition

```go
type RobotOutputConfig struct {
    Format     string `toml:"format"`  // "json" or "toon"
    Pretty     bool   `toml:"pretty"`
    Timestamps bool   `toml:"timestamps"`
    Compress   bool   `toml:"compress"`
}
```

## Validation
- format must be "json" or "toon"
- If format is "toon", check toon binary available

## Migration
Existing configs without this section default to JSON (backwards compatible).

## Acceptance Criteria

1. RobotOutputConfig struct added to config.go
2. [robot.output] section parsed from ntm.toml
3. Default values: format="json", pretty=false, timestamps=true, compress=false
4. Validation rejects invalid format values with clear error
5. Warning logged if format="toon" but toon binary not found
6. Existing configs without this section work unchanged

## Unit Tests (80% coverage target)
- internal/config/config_test.go
- Test parsing valid config
- Test default values
- Test validation errors

## E2E Test Script
```bash
#!/bin/bash
# [E2E-TOON-CONFIG] Robot Output Config Tests
LOG_PREFIX="[E2E-TOON-CONFIG]"
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Testing [robot.output] config"
# Create temp config with TOON format
mkdir -p /tmp/e2e-toon-test
echo '[robot.output]
format = "json"
pretty = true' > /tmp/e2e-toon-test/ntm.toml
# Verify config is parsed (would need ntm config command)
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Config parsing test requires ntm config command"
```","acceptance_criteria":"1. [robot.output] config section implemented
2. default_format option (json or toon)
3. Respects command-line override
4. Config validation on startup
5. Unit tests for config parsing","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T07:03:06.614723454Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T04:34:25.284883460Z","closed_at":"2026-01-23T04:34:25.284795364Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1u462","depends_on_id":"bd-6wc0a","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:25Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1u5g","title":"Unit Tests: Effectiveness metrics and scoring","description":"## Comprehensive Unit Test Suite for Effectiveness Scoring

### Test Coverage Required
1. **Metric definitions + weights** (`internal/scoring/metrics_test.go`)
   - Metric definitions are complete + stable
   - Weight normalization and recomputation
   - Edge cases: zero values, caps at 1.0

2. **ScoreTracker tests** (`internal/scoring/tracker_test.go`)
   - Record() writes valid JSONL
   - Retention pruning
   - Trend calculations (improving/declining/stable)
   - Deterministic ordering in summaries

3. **Derived scoring** (new tests in `internal/scoring/metrics_test.go` or `tracker_test.go`)
   - Overall score uses expected weights
   - Cold-start behavior (min samples)
   - Per-task-type weighting overrides

### Test Data
- Sample task completion data
- Various agent/task combinations
- Edge cases: insufficient data, outliers

### Logging
`t.Logf("SCORE_TEST: %s | Agent=%s | TaskType=%s | Score=%.2f", testName, agent, taskType, score)`

### Acceptance Criteria
- Tests are deterministic and fast (<5s)
- No filesystem pollution outside temp dirs
- JSONL round-trip verified","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T06:17:26.090041765Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T21:03:29.842379005Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1u5g","depends_on_id":"bd-1unv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:25Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1unv","title":"EPIC: Agent Effectiveness Scoring and Learning","description":"**Agent Effectiveness Scoring and Learning EPIC**

## What It Does
Track agent effectiveness over time and use that data to improve assignments and workflows.

## Architecture (Existing + Planned)
- **Metrics definitions:** `internal/scoring/metrics.go` (core metrics + weights)
- **Score persistence + trends:** `internal/scoring/tracker.go` (JSONL, retention, trend windows)
- **Integration:** `ntm assign` + dashboard panel + robot/analytics export

## Data Sources (Deterministic)
- Task lifecycle signals (beads: assigned → in_progress → closed)
- Retry counts (re-sent prompts / reassignments)
- Time-to-complete (timestamps in task + session logs)
- Token usage (BudgetTracker / TokenEstimator)
- Quality gates (tests/CI pass, regressions)

## Learning / Scoring Rules
- **Cold start:** fallback to neutral baseline until minimum sample threshold met
- **Stability:** use rolling window + decay (recent scores weighted higher)
- **Fairness:** per-task-type normalization to avoid skew from harder tasks
- **No gaming:** cap extreme values, validate sources, log anomalies

## Success Criteria
1. Per-agent metrics tracked and persisted (JSONL)
2. Task completion rates calculated
3. Effectiveness scores computed deterministically
4. Scores influence recommendations with explainable rationale
5. Dashboard shows agent rankings + trends
6. Robot mode exports scoring data
7. Unit tests for scoring algorithm (>=80% coverage)
8. E2E tests verify scoring accuracy + recommendation influence

## Notes
- Retention defaults to 90 days; configurable
- Scores must be reproducible for the same input set","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

 {"id":"bd-1uunz","title":"Implement agent launch sequence after exit","description":"Implement agent launch sequence after successful exit.

## Steps
1. Send 'cc' Enter to pane
2. Wait for claude code initialization (6 seconds typical)
3. Verify claude code is ready (check for prompt or ready indicator)

## Timing
- cc command: immediate
- Initialization wait: 6 seconds
- Ready check: poll for up to 10 seconds

## Detection
Look for:
- Claude Code prompt character
- 'Claude Code' banner text
- Ready indicator in output

## Error Handling
- If cc command fails to start: return error with shell output
- If initialization times out: return error with pane content","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:25:12.846348928Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T02:32:48.358201896Z","closed_at":"2026-01-22T02:32:48.358098031Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1uunz","depends_on_id":"bd-21tqw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:25Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-mk1i5","title":"Tier 1: Safety & Reliability Integrations","description":"EPIC for Tier 1 integrations focused on immediate safety and reliability improvements.

## Why Tier 1 is Highest Priority

These three integrations address the most critical pain points in multi-agent workflows:

1. **DCG**: Prevents catastrophic mistakes. A single 'rm -rf /' or 'git push --force origin main' from any agent can destroy hours of work or worse. DCG blocks these at the command level before they execute.

2. **CAAM**: Rate limits are the #1 operational pain point. When Claude hits rate limits, you wait hours. CAAM has OAuth tokens for multiple accounts and can switch in <100ms, letting work continue immediately.

3. **caut**: Cost visibility prevents bill shock. 16+ providers tracked. Know when you're approaching limits before you hit them.

## Integration Approach

All three are Rust CLIs that work via subprocess invocation:
- DCG: Hook into PreToolUse (already exists in claude-code ecosystem)
- CAAM: Add --robot-switch-account command to NTM robot mode
- caut: Add --robot-quota-status and dashboard widget

## Success Metrics

- [ ] Zero destructive commands execute across managed agents (DCG)
- [ ] Rate limit recovery time < 1 minute (CAAM)
- [ ] Quota visibility in dashboard with alerts at 80% threshold (caut)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-21T06:43:27.049682004Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T18:00:12.558020123Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T18:00:12.557947457Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-mk1i5","depends_on_id":"bd-3fg81","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:30Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-n5hu","title":"Task: Format and inject recovery prompt","description":"## Purpose
Synthesize all recovery context into a formatted prompt for agent injection.

## Recovery Prompt Template
# Session Recovery Context

## Your Previous Work
- You were working on: [bead title from BV]
- Last checkpoint: [timestamp] — [description]
- Files you were editing: [from Agent Mail reservations]

## Recent Messages
[Last 3 Agent Mail messages from relevant threads]

## Key Decisions Made
[From CM procedural memory for this project]

## Current Task Status
- [x] Completed: [completed beads]
- [ ] In progress: [in_progress beads]
- [ ] Blocked by: [blocking issues]

Reread AGENTS.md and continue from where you left off.

## Scope
- Format RecoveryContext into markdown prompt
- Apply token limit (config: max_recovery_tokens = 2000)
- Prioritize most relevant information if truncating
- Send to agent pane on spawn

## Acceptance Criteria
- Recovery prompt readable and actionable
- Respects token limit configuration
- Graceful when partial context available
- Sent before user's first prompt

## Technical Notes
Use template/text for formatting
Send via existing send mechanism","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T05:35:17.291725081Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T03:43:05.409285011Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T03:43:05.409240778Z","close_reason":"Implementation complete: FormatRecoveryPrompt function synthesizes context into markdown, token limits enforced via truncateRecoveryContext, graceful degradation tested, injection happens on spawn.  {"id":"bd-n770","title":"Add YAML swarm config file - define entire swarm (agents, prompts, assignments) in one declarative file","description":"## Overview
Define entire swarm configuration in one declarative YAML file.

## Config Format (swarm.yaml)
```yaml
name: myproject-swarm
session: myproject

agents:
  claude: 10
  codex: 10  
  gemini: 4
  
options:
  no-user: true
  auto-assign: true
  
prompts:
  init: |
    Read AGENTS.md and register with Agent Mail.
    Check your inbox and respond to any messages.
    Use bv --robot-triage to find work.
  review: |
    Review code written by fellow agents...
  post-compaction: |
    Reread AGENTS.md. Use ultrathink.

assignment:
  source: bv
  limit: 24
  template: impl
  round-robin: false
  
monitor:
  stuck_threshold_seconds: 300
  auto_restart_stuck: true
  completion_patterns:
    - 'bead complete'
    - 'task finished'
```

## Commands
```bash
ntm swarm --config=swarm.yaml           # Start full swarm
ntm spawn --config=swarm.yaml           # Just spawn from config
ntm assign --config=swarm.yaml          # Use assignment settings
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Parses YAML config format
- [ ] Validates required fields
- [ ] Supports all spawn options
- [ ] Supports prompt templates
- [ ] Supports assignment settings
- [ ] Supports monitor settings
- [ ] Clear error messages for invalid config

## Tests Required
- Unit test: YAML parsing and validation
- Unit test: Config defaults
- E2E test: Full swarm from config file","notes":"Closed as dropped: avoid new YAML format; reuse existing pipeline/recipe configs if declarative setup is needed.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-19T08:40:47.613482061Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T20:16:34.562867377Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T19:09:17.646542265Z","close_reason":"Dropped per canonical assign flow; redundant/overlapping entry points","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-n770","depends_on_id":"bd-2rqt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:31Z","created_by":"import"}]}
